questions.text: What is unique about the beginning of Surah At-Tawbah?
questions.answer: It is the only Surah in the Quran that does not begin with 'Bismillah-ir-Rahman-ir-Rahim'.

questions.text: Why is there no 'Bismillah' at the start of this Surah?
questions.answer: Mainly because it is a continuation of Surah Al-Anfal, and it begins with a declaration of 'Bar'at' (disassociation/ultimatum), which is not compatible with the theme of 'Mercy' in the Basmalah.

questions.text: What is the 'Bara'ah' mentioned at the start of the Surah?
questions.answer: A declaration of disassociation from the polytheists by Allah and His Messenger regarding the treaties they had violated.

questions.text: What was the 'respite' period given to the mushrikeen to travel freely in the land?
questions.answer: Four months.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Hajj' (Al-Hajj al-Akbar) mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: It refers to the day of the major pilgrimage in the 9th year of Hijrah when this declaration was announced to the people.

questions.text: What is the ruling for those mushrikeen who did NOT break their treaty with the believers?
questions.answer: The believers are commanded to fulfill their treaty with them until the end of its term.

questions.text: If a polytheist seeks asylum (protection) from a believer, what should be done?
questions.answer: The believer must grant him protection so that he may hear the word of Allah, and then escort him to his place of safety.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the reliability of the disbelievers' oaths and treaties?
questions.answer: They do not respect any kinship or treaty with a believer; they please you with their mouths, but their hearts refuse, and most of them are defiantly disobedient.

questions.text: What is the requirement for someone to be allowed to maintain the mosques of Allah?
questions.answer: They must believe in Allah and the Last Day, establish prayer, give Zakat, and fear none but Allah.

questions.text: Is providing water to pilgrims or maintaining the Sacred Mosque equal to Jihad in the cause of Allah?
questions.answer: No, those who believe and fight in the cause of Allah have a much higher rank in the sight of Allah.

questions.text: What is the warning regarding putting love for family, wealth, or business above love for Allah and His Messenger?
questions.answer: If these things are more beloved to you than Allah, His Messenger, and Jihad in His cause, then wait until Allah brings about His command (punishment).

questions.text: What lesson did the believers learn at the Battle of Hunayn?
questions.answer: That their great numbers did not avail them at all when they were amazed by their own strength; victory comes only from Allah.

questions.text: Why were the mushrikeen forbidden from approaching the Sacred Mosque (Al-Masjid al-Haram) after that year?
questions.answer: Because they are considered 'Najas' (spiritually impure) due to their shirk.

questions.text: What is the 'Jizyah' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: A tax/tribute paid by the People of the Book living under Islamic protection in lieu of military service.

questions.text: What claim did the Jews make about Uzair (Ezra) and the Christians about Isa (Jesus)?
questions.answer: The Jews said Uzair is the son of Allah, and the Christians said the Messiah is the son of Allah.

questions.text: How did the People of the Book treat their rabbis and monks according to this Surah?
questions.answer: They took them as 'lords' besides Allah by following their changes to the divine laws.

questions.text: What is the punishment for those who hoard gold and silver and do not spend it in the way of Allah?
questions.answer: On the Day of Judgment, their wealth will be heated in the fire of Hell and used to brand their foreheads, sides, and backs.

questions.text: How many months are in a year according to Allah's decree, and how many are sacred?
questions.answer: Twelve months; four of them are sacred.

questions.text: Which expedition (battle) does this Surah primarily discuss, occurring in extreme heat?
questions.answer: The expedition to Tabuk.

questions.text: What is the warning for those who hesitate to go out in the cause of Allah when called?
questions.answer: If they do not go forth, Allah will punish them with a painful punishment and replace them with another people.

questions.text: What is 'Al-Ghar-ul-Thani' (the second of the two) referring to in the context of the Hijrah?
questions.answer: The Prophet (SAW) and Abu Bakr (RA) in the cave of Thawr, when the Prophet said, 'Do not grieve; indeed Allah is with us.'

questions.text: What was the excuse of some hypocrites for not joining the Tabuk expedition?
questions.answer: Some said the heat was too intense, while others made up false personal excuses.

questions.text: What does Allah say about those who 'laughed much' and refused to fight?
questions.answer: They should laugh little and weep much as a recompense for what they used to earn.

questions.text: Are believers allowed to pray over the dead bodies of hypocrites?
questions.answer: No, the Prophet was commanded never to pray over any of them who died or stand at their graves.

questions.text: What was the reaction of the sincere believers who could not join the expedition because they had no means of transport?
questions.answer: Their eyes overflowed with tears out of grief that they could not find anything to spend.

questions.text: What are the eight categories of people entitled to receive Zakat (a specific ayah)?
questions.answer: The poor, the needy, those who collect Zakat, those whose hearts are to be reconciled, for freeing slaves, those in debt, in the cause of Allah, and the traveler.

questions.text: How do the hypocrites (men and women) behave toward each other?
questions.answer: They enjoin what is wrong and forbid what is right and close their hands (are stingy).

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the relationship between male and female believers?
questions.answer: They are 'Auliya' (allies/protectors) of one another, enjoining right and forbidding wrong, establishing prayer, and giving Zakat.

questions.text: What is 'Adn' mentioned in the rewards for the believers?
questions.answer: Gardens of perpetual residence (Eden) beneath which rivers flow.

questions.text: What is the 'greatest' reward from Allah mentioned for the believers in Paradise?
questions.answer: The pleasure (Ridwan) of Allah is the greatest reward.

questions.text: What did the hypocrites do regarding their promises to give charity if Allah made them rich?
questions.answer: When He gave them of His bounty, they became stingy and turned away, so He put hypocrisy in their hearts until the Day they meet Him.

questions.text: How did the hypocrites mock the poor believers who gave charity?
questions.answer: They ridiculed those who gave much and mocked those who could find nothing to give but their own effort.

questions.text: Will seeking forgiveness for the hypocrites help them?
questions.answer: Allah told the Prophet that even if he asked forgiveness for them seventy times, Allah would never forgive them because they disbelieved in Allah and His Messenger.

questions.text: Who are the 'desert Arabs' (Bedouins) according to the Surah?
questions.answer: Some are described as the intensest in disbelief and hypocrisy, while others believe in Allah and the Last Day and view their spending as a means of nearness to Allah.

questions.text: Who are the 'Sabiqun al-Awwalun'?
questions.answer: The first to embrace Islam among the Muhajirun (emigrants from Makkah) and the Ansar (helpers of Madinah).

questions.text: What should be done with those who acknowledge their sins and have mixed a righteous deed with another that was evil?
questions.answer: Perhaps Allah will turn to them in forgiveness; charity should be taken from their wealth to purify them.

questions.text: What was 'Masjid al-Dirar'?
questions.answer: The 'Mosque of Harm' built by hypocrites to cause division and serve as a base for enemies; Allah commanded the Prophet (SAW) never to stand in it.

questions.text: Which mosque is described as being 'founded on righteousness (Taqwa) from the first day'?
questions.answer: The Mosque of Quba (though some scholars say it also refers to the Prophet's Mosque in Madinah).

questions.text: What is the 'trade' that Allah has made with the believers?
questions.answer: Allah has purchased from the believers their lives and their properties in exchange for Paradise.

questions.text: Is it permissible for the Prophet and the believers to seek forgiveness for the polytheists?
questions.answer: No, not even if they are near relatives, after it has become clear that they are companions of Hellfire.

questions.text: Why did Ibrahim (AS) seek forgiveness for his father, and why did he eventually stop?
questions.answer: He only did it because of a promise he had made; once it became clear that his father was an enemy to Allah, he disassociated himself from him.

questions.text: What is the significance of 'The Three who were left behind'?
questions.answer: Three sincere believers who missed the Tabuk expedition without a valid excuse; they were boycotted for 50 days until Allah revealed their repentance was accepted.

questions.text: How did the 'Three' feel during the period of their boycott?
questions.answer: The earth, vast as it is, became straitened for them, and their own souls became straitened.

questions.text: What is the command regarding being with the 'truthful' (Sadiqin)?
questions.answer: Believers are commanded to fear Allah and be with those who are true in word and deed.

questions.text: Does any act of thirst, hunger, or exhaustion in the cause of Allah go unrewarded?
questions.answer: No, every such act is recorded for the believers as a righteous deed.

questions.text: Should all the believers go out to fight at once?
questions.answer: No, a group from every division should remain behind to obtain a deep understanding of the religion and to warn their people when they return.

questions.text: What effect do the Surahs of the Quran have on the believers vs the hypocrites?
questions.answer: They increase the believers in faith, but for those with diseased hearts, they increase them in filth (disbelief) upon filth.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the Prophet's feelings toward the believers' suffering?
questions.answer: It grieves him that they should suffer; he is deeply concerned for them and is kind and merciful to the believers.

questions.text: What is the final advice given to the Prophet if people turn away from the message?
questions.answer: To say: 'Sufficient for me is Allah; there is no deity except Him. On Him I have relied, and He is the Lord of the Great Throne.'

questions.text: What is the 'sacredness' of the four months mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Wrongdoing (sin) is considered more grievous during these months, and they were traditionally months of no fighting.

questions.text: What is the punishment for those who mock the verses of Allah and His Messenger?
questions.answer: They have disbelieved after their belief, and unless they repent, they will face a painful punishment.

questions.text: What is the 'Mosque of Taqwa'?
questions.answer: A mosque built with the intention of pleasing Allah and establishing prayer from the very first day.

questions.text: What is the 'Hardship' (Al-'Usrah) mentioned in the context of Tabuk?
questions.answer: It refers to the extreme difficulty of the expedition due to distance, heat, and lack of resources.

questions.text: Who are the 'Mu'allafati Qulubuhum' in the distribution of Zakat?
questions.answer: Those whose hearts are to be reconciled to Islam or whose support is sought for the community.

questions.text: What is the ruling on the 'Nasi' (postponement of sacred months) practiced by the polytheists?
questions.answer: It is an increase in disbelief by which the disbelievers are further led astray.

questions.text: How many years did the disbelievers have to travel freely after the declaration of Bara'ah?
questions.answer: Actually, the respite was specifically for four months from the day of the announcement.

questions.text: What did the hypocrites say about the Prophet being 'all ear' (listening to everyone)?
questions.answer: They tried to insult him, but Allah said he is 'an ear of goodness' for the believers.

questions.text: What is the description of the 'Fire of Hell' regarding the wealth of the stingy?
questions.answer: It will be used to brand their foreheads, sides, and backs, and they will be told: 'This is what you hoarded for yourselves.'

questions.text: What is the 'Sakinah' that Allah sent down in the cave?
questions.answer: Tranquility and peace that allowed the Prophet to remain calm despite the danger.

questions.text: What is the 'unseen army' that supported the Prophet during the Hijrah?
questions.answer: The angels of Allah.

questions.text: How does Allah view the repentance of the Prophet, Muhajirun, and Ansar?
questions.answer: He has already accepted their repentance and is pleased with them.

questions.text: What is the 'word of Allah' described as in the context of the Hijrah?
questions.answer: It is the highest and supreme word.

questions.text: What is the fate of the hypocrite men and women in the Hereafter?
questions.answer: They are promised the Fire of Hell, to abide therein forever; it is sufficient for them.

questions.text: What is the 'noble provision' (Rizqun Karim)?
questions.answer: The blessings and rewards of Paradise.

questions.text: How should the believers treat the hypocrites who refused to join them once they returned?
questions.answer: They should turn away from them as they are an abomination.

questions.text: What is the reward for the Muhajirun and Ansar?
questions.answer: Gardens beneath which rivers flow and the eternal pleasure of Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'purification' (Tathir) mentioned regarding charity?
questions.answer: Charity purifies the wealth and the soul of the giver from stinginess and sins.

questions.text: Who is the 'best builder' mentioned in the context of the mosques?
questions.answer: The one who builds on the foundation of Taqwa and the pleasure of Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'collapsing cliff' a metaphor for?
questions.answer: The foundation of the hypocrites' mosque, which leads them into the fire of Hell.

questions.text: What is the 'Good News' (Bushra) for the believers?
questions.answer: The promise of Paradise and success from Allah.

questions.text: Why did Ibrahim (AS) eventually disassociate from his father?
questions.answer: Because he realized his father was a permanent enemy to Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Deep Understanding' (Tafaqquh) of religion?
questions.answer: Gaining a profound knowledge of Allah's laws and guidance to teach others.

questions.text: What is the 'kindness and mercy' (Ra'uf, Rahim) of the Prophet?
questions.answer: Attributes of Allah that are also used to describe the Prophet's character toward the believers.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Throne' (Al-'Arsh al-'Azim)?
questions.answer: The magnificent throne of Allah, signifying His supreme sovereignty.

questions.text: What is the 'Ultimatum' in the context of this Surah?
questions.answer: The declaration of the end of peace treaties with those who violated them.

questions.text: What is the 'Light of Allah' that the disbelievers try to extinguish?
questions.answer: The message of Islam and the Quran.

questions.text: What is the 'Grievous Punishment' (Adhabun Alim)?
questions.answer: The severe punishment in Hell for those who reject faith.

questions.text: What is the 'Success' (Fawz) mentioned multiple times in the Surah?
questions.answer: Attaining Allah's pleasure and Paradise.

questions.text: Why is the Surah titled 'At-Tawbah' (The Repentance)?
questions.answer: Because it emphasizes Allah's acceptance of the repentance of the believers and the three who were left behind.

questions.text: What is the final message of the Surah regarding reliance?
questions.answer: That a believer should rely only on Allah, for He is sufficient for all needs.
